summary: Gruber excoriates Jeff Bezos and Washington Post publisher William Lewis for killing the paper's presidential endorsement of Kamala Harris, days after LA Times owner Patrick Soon-Shiong made a similar move. He dismantles Lewis's claim that this is a return to pre-1976 tradition, noting the Post began endorsing precisely because of Watergate. Gruber assembles a devastating chorus of condemnation from Post insiders including Woodward and Bernstein, former editor Marty Baron, and 17 current columnists. He argues the non-endorsements are worse than forced Trump endorsements because they create plausible suspicion, and frames Bezos's capitulation as anticipatory obedience to an authoritarian threat. Gruber announces he has cancelled his Post subscription and urges readers to do the same.
key_points: Lewis's justification of returning to pre-1976 'roots' is dismantled by the fact that the Post started endorsing specifically because of Watergate — reported by its own journalists
The non-endorsement is more damaging than a forced Trump endorsement would be, because it creates plausible suspicion that even liberal papers won't back Harris
Bezos didn't need to be arrested or threatened — the mere possibility of a Trump presidency was enough to secure his compliance, illustrating 'anticipatory obedience'
The Post's own journalists revolted publicly: Woodward and Bernstein, 17 current columnists, and former editor Marty Baron all condemned the decision
Gruber questions what the point of 'fuck you money' is if you're too afraid to use it against a petty tyrant
money_quotes: The only rational explanation for this decision is cowardice on Bezos's part in the face of Donald Trump's vindictiveness.
Liberal newspapers breaking tradition to not endorse anyone is worse than if their owners had forced them to endorse Trump instead.
A Trump endorsement from the LA Times or Washington Post would be absurd. No one, not even the derpiest of MAGA trolls, would believe that. It would be like a steakhouse endorsing veganism.
What's the point of having so much 'fuck you' money if you're afraid to tell a petty tyrant like Donald Trump to pound sand?
Transactions work only one way with Donald Trump. Toward him.
This is no time to get squishy.
I've been a paying subscriber to The Washington Post for many years. Not anymore.
key_insight: Bezos killing the Post's endorsement before Trump even won the election demonstrates that authoritarianism's power is freely given through anticipatory obedience, and the supposed institutional guardrails are just people who are already collapsing.
